bibliographic_data.title: Model-plant mismatch learning offset-free model predictive control
bibliographic_data.abstract: We propose model-plant mismatch learning offset-free model predictive control (MPC), which learns and applies the intrinsic model-plant mismatch, to effectively exploit the advantages of model-based and data-driven control strategies and overcome the limitations of each approach. In this study, the model-plant mismatch map on steady-state manifold in the controlled variable space is approximated via a general regression neural network from the steady-state data for each setpoint. Though the learned model-plant mismatch map can provide the information at the equilibrium point (i.e., setpoint), it cannot provide model-plant mismatch information during the transient state. Moreover, the intrinsic model-plant mismatch can vary due to system characteristics changes during operation. Therefore, we additionally apply a supplementary disturbance variable which is updated from the disturbance estimator based on the nominal offset-free MPC scheme. Then, the combined disturbance signal is applied to the target problem and finite-horizon optimal control problem of offset-free MPC to improve the prediction accuracy and closed-loop performance of the controller. By this, we can exploit both the learned model-plant mismatch information and the stabilizing property of the nominal disturbance estimator approach. The closed-loop simulation results demonstrate that the developed scheme can properly learn the intrinsic model-plant mismatch and efficiently improve the model-plant mismatch compensating performance in offset-free MPC. Moreover, we examine the robust asymptotic stability of the developed offset-free MPC scheme, which is known to be difficult to analyze in nominal offset-free MPC, by exploiting the learned model-plant mismatch information.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proposes a method that combines model-based and data-driven control strategies to improve the performance of offset-free MPC by learning and applying the intrinsic model-plant mismatch.
core_content.key_methodology: A general regression neural network (GRNN) is used to approximate the model-plant mismatch map on the steady-state manifold in the controlled variable space. A supplementary disturbance signal, updated from a disturbance estimator based on nominal offset-free MPC, is applied during transient states and system characteristics changes.
outcomes: The proposed scheme can learn and apply the intrinsic model-plant mismatch to improve prediction accuracy and closed-loop performance of the controller.
The robust asymptotic stability of the developed offset-free MPC scheme is examined by exploiting learned model-plant mismatch information.
core_content.full_text_markdown: In this paper, we propose a method that combines model-based and data-driven control strategies to improve the performance of offset-free Model Predictive Control (MPC). The key novelty lies in learning and applying the intrinsic model-plant mismatch. Specifically, a general regression neural network is used to approximate the model-plant mismatch map on the steady-state manifold in the controlled variable space from steady-state data for each setpoint. During transient states or when system characteristics change, a supplementary disturbance signal derived from a disturbance estimator based on nominal offset-free MPC is applied. This combined approach aims to improve prediction accuracy and closed-loop performance of the controller. The paper also examines the robust asymptotic stability of the developed scheme by utilizing learned model-plant mismatch information.
